messages.content:
This line

	GetMesh()->SetCollisionResponseToChannel(ECC_Interactable, ECR_Block)

is giving me the error 

Severity	Code	Description	Project	File	Line	Suppression State
Error (active)	E0020	identifier "ECC_Interactable" is undefined	ProjectNova	C:\Users\44793\Desktop\Unreal Engine Files\Unreal Engine 5.3\ProjectNova\Source\ProjectNova\Private\Character\NovaEnemyCharacter.cpp	9

The error message indicates that the identifier "ECC_Interactable" is not recognized in the given context. This usually occurs when the necessary header file is not included or the custom enumeration is not accessible.

To resolve this issue, ensure that you have included the header file where the custom enumeration `ECustomCollisionChannel` is defined. In this case, it seems that the header file where `ECustomCollisionChannel` is defined is missing from your `NovaEnemyCharacter.cpp` file.

Make sure you include the appropriate header file at the top of `NovaEnemyCharacter.cpp` file, like this:

```cpp
#include "YourCustomHeaderFileName.h" // Replace with the actual header file name containing ECustomCollisionChannel
```

Also, ensure that the custom enumeration is accessible/imported in the `NovaEnemyCharacter.cpp` file. Double-check that you have included the correct header file and that it is properly set up.

By including the correct header file and ensuring the custom enumeration is accessible, the identifier "ECC_Interactable" should be recognized in the given context without any errors.
How do I define the variable?

To define a variable using the custom collision channel `ECC_Interactable`, you can declare a variable of type `TEnumAsByte<ECustomCollisionChannel>`. Here's an example:

```cpp
TEnumAsByte<ECustomCollisionChannel> MyCustomCollisionChannel = ECustomCollisionChannel::ECC_Interactable;
```

In this example, `MyCustomCollisionChannel` is a variable of type `TEnumAsByte<ECustomCollisionChannel>`, and it is assigned the value `ECC_Interactable`.

You can then make use of this custom collision channel variable in your code according to your requirements. For example, to set the collision response of a mesh component to `ECC_Interactable`, you can do something like this:

```cpp
GetMesh()->SetCollisionResponseToChannel(MyCustomCollisionChannel.GetValue(), ECR_Block);
```

Make sure to replace `MyCustomCollisionChannel` with the actual variable name you are using in your code.

By defining and using the variable in this way, you can work with the custom collision channel `ECC_Interactable` in your Unreal Engine C++ code.
Okay, for the CustomCollisionChannel what do I need to include in the header?

To include the custom collision channel `ECustomCollisionChannel` in your header file, you need to add the following line:

```cpp
#include "YourCustomHeaderFileName.h" // Replace with the actual header file name where ECustomCollisionChannel is defined
```

In the line above, you should replace "YourCustomHeaderFileName.h" with the actual header file name where you have defined the `ECustomCollisionChannel` enumeration.

For example, if you have defined the `ECustomCollisionChannel` enum in a header file called "CustomCollisionChannels.h", you would include it as follows:

```cpp
#include "CustomCollisionChannels.h"
```

By including the appropriate header file, you make the `ECustomCollisionChannel` enum visible and accessible in your current header file, allowing you to use it to define and work with variables of that type.

I'm trying to define 

TEnumAsByte<ECustomCollisionChannel> Interactable = ECustomCollisionChannel::ECC_Interactable;

inside my main project header file (ProjectNova.h)

However I'm getting the error

Severity	Code	Description	Project	File	Line	Suppression State
Error (active)	E0020	identifier "ECustomCollisionChannel" is undefined	ProjectNova	C:\Users\44793\Desktop\Unreal Engine Files\Unreal Engine 5.3\ProjectNova\Source\ProjectNova\ProjectNova.h	7

Based on the error message you provided, it seems that the compiler cannot find the definition of the `ECustomCollisionChannel` enum in your `ProjectNova.h` file.

To resolve this issue, you need to make sure that the `#include` directive for the header file where `ECustomCollisionChannel` is defined is present at the top of your `ProjectNova.h` file. 

For example, if the `ECustomCollisionChannel` enum is defined in a header file called "CustomCollisionChannels.h", you would include it in your `ProjectNova.h` file like this:

```cpp
#include "CustomCollisionChannels.h" // Replace "CustomCollisionChannels.h" with the actual header file name
```

Make sure to replace `"CustomCollisionChannels.h"` with the actual header file name that contains the definition of `ECustomCollisionChannel`. 

By including the correct header file, the compiler will be able to find the definition of `ECustomCollisionChannel`, and you should no longer encounter the "identifier is undefined" error.

Does this:

#define ECC_Interactable ECC_GameTraceChannel1

allow me to call ECC_Interactable to get ECC_GameTraceChannel1

It seems there is a typo in your code. In this line:

```cpp
GetMesh()->SetCollisionResponseToChannel(ECC_Interatable, ECR_Block);
```

The enum `ECC_Interatable` appears to have a typo. It should be `ECC_Interactable`, matching the custom collision channel you defined earlier. So, the corrected line of code should be:

```cpp
GetMesh()->SetCollisionResponseToChannel(ECC_Interactable, ECR_Block);
```

With this correction, the `SetCollisionResponseToChannel` function should work as expected and set the collision response to block the `ECC_Interactable` trace channel for the `Mesh` component.

Please update your code with this correction, and it should properly block the `ECC_Interactable` trace channel in your blueprints.
If I created a Custom Trace channel in the project settings, would it be using ECC_GameTraceChannel1? or the ECC_EngineTraceChannel1?

If you create a custom trace channel in the Project Settings of Unreal Engine, it will not be using either `ECC_GameTraceChannel1` or `ECC_EngineTraceChannel1` by default. 

By creating a custom trace channel, you define your own unique collision channel that can be used for traces and collision handling within your project. When creating a custom trace channel, you can specify its name, response, and visibility settings based on your project requirements.

The `ECC_GameTraceChannel1` and `ECC_EngineTraceChannel1` are predefined trace channel values provided by Unreal Engine, but they are separate from any custom trace channels you create in the Project Settings. Each custom trace channel that you create will have its own unique identifier and collision settings.

To use your custom trace channel in code, you would typically refer to it by its specified name, not by the predefined trace channel values like `ECC_GameTraceChannel1`. You can access the custom trace channel by its name and set the collision response accordingly using the `SetCollisionResponseToChannel` function.

For example:

```cpp
// Assuming you have created a custom trace channel named "MyCustomTraceChannel" in the Project Settings
GetMesh()->SetCollisionResponseToChannel(ECollisionChannel::ECC_CustomTraceChannel1, ECR_Block);
```

Replace "MyCustomTraceChannel" with the actual name of your custom trace channel defined in the Project Settings.

Remember to specify the correct collision response (`ECR_Block`, `ECR_Overlap`, etc.) based on your desired behavior for that custom trace channel.
